mainEntity.name: What documents does a foreigner need for GST registration?

acceptedAnswer.text: Passport copy, Tax Identification Number from home country, authorised signatory details (Indian resident), business address proof in India, and bank account details.

mainEntity.name: How long is the registration valid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Valid for the period specified in the application (minimum 1 day, maximum 90 days). Can be extended by filing a fresh application before expiry.
Yes. After final return (GSTR-5) is filed and all liabilities are settled, the excess advance deposit is refundable.

mainEntity.name: Can a foreign company register for GST in India?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Foreign companies supplying goods/services in India need GST registration. Can register as: (1) Indian subsidiary, (2) through authorised agent/representative, or (3) as non-resident taxable person (for temporary supply).

mainEntity.name: Does a foreign company need PAN for GST?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. PAN is mandatory for regular GST registration. Foreign companies must first obtain PAN from Indian income tax authorities. For temporary registration: TIN (Tax Identification Number) may suffice.

mainEntity.name: What is non-resident taxable person registration?

acceptedAnswer.text: Temporary GST registration for foreign entities making occasional taxable supply in India. Valid for 90 days (extendable). Must deposit estimated tax in advance. No permanent establishment required.

mainEntity.name: Is GST registration needed for software services from abroad?

acceptedAnswer.text: If foreign company provides services to Indian businesses: IGST applicable under reverse charge (Indian recipient pays). Foreign company does not need GST registration. If providing to Indian consumers: registration may be needed.

mainEntity.name: What documents are needed for foreign entity GST?

acceptedAnswer.text: Passport of authorised person, PAN of Indian entity/representative, authorisation letter, address proof of Indian business place, bank account details of Indian bank, and digital signature of authorised signatory.
